integration.title: Platform Integration Guide
integration.description: This guide explains how to integrate your platform with the agent system for member management, transaction recording, and reward distribution.
integration.apiKeyAuthDesc: Authenticate via X-API-Key header. Each platform has its own key.
integration.idempotentDesc: Registration and transaction APIs support idempotent operations. Retries won't create duplicates.
integration.unifiedResponseDesc: All APIs return a consistent JSON structure with success / code / message / data.
integration.getApiKeyDesc: Contact your admin to obtain your platform API Key. Each platform has its own key for all API request authentication.
integration.apiKeyWarning: Your API Key is equivalent to a password. Never expose it in frontend code or public Git repos. Store it in environment variables or a Secret Manager.
integration.memberRegisterDesc: Call this API when a user completes registration or first login on your platform to sync them to the agent system.
integration.memberRegisterIdempotent: If keycloak_id already exists, the API returns the existing member data without creating duplicates. Safe to retry.
integration.recordTransactionDesc: Call this API when a user completes a purchase on your platform. The system auto-calculates commissions and points.
integration.transactionIdempotent: Use your system's order ID as external_tx_id. Duplicate submissions return the existing record with isDuplicate: true.
integration.queryBalanceDesc: Query a member's points and commission balance anytime, for displaying reward info on your platform.
integration.queryBalanceTip: Call this API on member center, checkout, or profile pages to display real-time points and commission balance.
integration.responseFormatDesc: All API responses follow this JSON structure, regardless of success or failure:
integration.errorExampleDesc: Recommended: handle errors uniformly based on the code field:
integration.faqApiKey: How do I get an API Key?
integration.faqApiKeyAnswer: Contact your admin with your platform name and contact info. They'll create a platform and issue an API Key. Each platform has its own key.
integration.faqPlatformIdAnswer: Each integrated platform is assigned a unique platform_id to identify transaction sources. It must match the API Key. Get it when the admin issues your key.
integration.faqKeycloakId: What is keycloak_id and where do I get it?
integration.faqKeycloakIdAnswer: It's the user's unique identifier (UUID) in the Keycloak SSO system. When users log in via Keycloak on your platform, get it from the token's sub claim.
integration.faqDuplicateAnswer: The transaction API supports idempotent operations. Duplicate external_tx_id submissions return the existing record with isDuplicate: true. Use your system's order ID as external_tx_id.
integration.faqReferral: How does the referral system work?
integration.faqReferralAnswer: Each member gets an 8-character referral code after registration. When a new member registers with a referral_code, the system auto-creates the referral relationship and awards both parties (points or commission).
integration.faqRateLimitAnswer: Currently no rate limits, but please keep request frequency reasonable. Future limits may return code: 429 when exceeded.
integration.faqTestEnv: How to integrate in a test environment?
integration.faqTestEnvAnswer: Your admin can create a test platform and API Key. Test and production use the same API interface; just swap the API Key and Base URL.
integration.faqTimestampAnswer: Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Use new Date(timestamp) to convert to a Date object.
integration.needHelpDesc: Check the full API specification or contact your admin for technical support.
integration.referralCodeField: Referral code, both parties earn rewards
integration.platformIdField: Your platform ID (must match API Key)
integration.code400Cause: Missing required fields, format error, negative amount
